title: thymelaeaceae
intro: The Thymelaeaceae rank refers to a family of flowering plants, commonly known as the thyme-leaved family or the daphne family. This family consists of around 30-40 genera, which include species such as Thymelaea, Daphne, and Stellera. The Thymelaeaceae family is characterized by their unique combination of characteristics, including small, fragrant flowers and leathery or deciduous leaves. Plants within this family are commonly found in a wide range of habitats, from tropical forests to arctic tundras.
